name: Aurum app
message: Aurum has a fresh update ready. Refresh to load the newest calculator and Hive improvements.
updates: Update detection has been bumped with fresh app, Hive, script, and offline cache versions so users receive the newest release notification.
[NEW] Hive account profiles now include an Email field that saves locally, imports and exports with JSON, and syncs to the cloud after the latest schema is applied.
On mobile, tapping Go to from Hive search results now closes the panel and centers the requested account on the canvas.
The Hive now stands out in the hero settings menu with a gold featured action style.
The hero settings menu now uses a more opaque dark surface and stronger row contrast so mobile menu items stay readable over the bright hero background.
[NEW] Mobile users can now install Aurum as a home-screen app, with a mobile install prompt and offline app shell caching.
Hive PIN changes now require confirmation and show a keep-your-PIN-safe warning.
Referral links now support native sharing when available, with copy fallback.
Hive PDF exports now include the selected account referral link and QR code when available.
Hive QR display now shows a fallback note if the QR image cannot load.
Hive search results now offer Show all when more than eight accounts match.
Cloud sync status now clearly shows when local changes are saved but cloud sync is still pending.
[NEW] Hive Starter setup can configure a root account, optional PIN, and three placeholder subaccounts in one guided flow.
The hero Hive background visual has been moved left so it no longer sits behind the Aurum wordmark.
The hero now includes a subtle scientific calculator, graph, and Hive structure visual layer.
The main hero title now says ROI Calculator, with a subtle calculator-style background hint in the header.
On mobile, the Hive account card now starts collapsed when the Hive first opens.
[NEW] Referral links can now reveal a scannable QR code from the selected account summary card.
Hive search results now include a Clear button to reset the result list and remove the pulse highlight.
The Hive account card is now collapsible and shows the selected account name in its header.
[NEW] Hive search now shows a results panel with account name, Referral ID, type, country, and Go to actions.
[NEW] Hive account PINs now use dedicated Change PIN and Remove PIN actions instead of editing the PIN in the account form.
Hive cloud sync status now reads as a clearer health indicator with friendlier wording.
Hive mobile layout now has larger touch controls, better toolbar wrapping, and tighter account-panel spacing.
Hive sync messages now use user-friendly cloud database wording instead of backend provider names.
Adding a child under a PIN-protected non-root Hive account now requires that account PIN, while the loaded root stays unlocked after the initial load verification.
PIN-protected Hive accounts now require the PIN before the account editor can be unlocked for changes.
[NEW] Protected Hive accounts now use a polished PIN unlock screen with four PIN slots and a phone-style keypad.
Hive lookup now shows the hourglass Loading state again while Referral IDs and PIN protection are checked.
Hive name search now makes the matched account pulse larger and brighter so it is easier to spot.
[NEW] Hive accounts can now have an optional 4-digit PIN privacy gate, with PIN prompts shown only after a protected Referral ID is found.
Cloud database schema now adds and validates an optional account access PIN without requiring destructive migrations.
Hive update reload now uses a cache-busted refresh and returns to the Hive without repeatedly showing the same update loop.
Hive Summary and Hive actions now have stronger collapsed-section styling so they read as expandable panel areas.
Hive legend now includes the white question-mark placeholder subaccount marker.
Auto-created Hive subaccounts now show as white question-mark placeholders until the user replaces their name and generated Referral ID.
Legacy Hive prototypes and support snippets were moved into docs/legacy-hive so the app root is easier to maintain.
Cloud database schema now has an additive hardening path for trimmed account text, non-negative money fields, and faster update lookups.
Hive import and cloud sync now validate structure integrity before accepting or publishing account data.
Export and price alert reminders now use visible app toasts instead of disruptive browser alerts.
ROI Calculator now uses a shared calculation core with regression tests covering compounding, simple mode, switches, deposits, and variance.
ROI Calculator simple-mode charts now distinguish total value from active balance, so profits that are set aside are visible instead of looking like compounded balance growth.
[NEW] The footer now includes a Changelog button that opens the release notes at any time.
[NEW] Changelog entries can now show a NEW badge for newly added functionality, separate from fixes and behavior updates.

ROI Calculator mixed simple/compound projections now keep simple-mode profits in the projected value after switching back to compounding, and grouped percentages account for deposits inside the period.
[NEW] ROI Calculator deposit rows can now be one-time or monthly recurring, with a configurable number of months.
[NEW] ROI Calculator now supports multiple additional deposits on specific days or dates, including saved scenarios, charts, and exports.
[NEW] Hive search now matches both account names and Referral IDs.
Update dialog actions now stay visible while long change logs scroll, and Hive sync log names display beside Referral IDs.
Deleting unfunded sub accounts now targets only the selected account and confirms with Referral ID and name.
Hive sync log entries now include account names alongside Referral IDs.
Release log refreshed for the protected Hive Save or Cancel workflow across add and edit modes.
Hive Edit selected now has a protected Save or Cancel flow that keeps canvas selection and sync paused while editing.
Hive add now checks the cloud database for duplicate Referral IDs before saving the new account and any auto-created subaccounts.
Hive add mode now requires Save or Cancel before changing canvas selection, and sync pauses while a new account is being entered.
[NEW] Adding a main Hive account can now auto-create three linked subaccounts using the main Referral ID plus -1, -2, and -3.
[NEW] Hive now keeps a local sync log showing added, removed, and edited accounts detected during cloud sync.
Hive auto-sync now refreshes on realtime events, focus return, and timer polling with a visible last-sync timestamp.
Hive account saves now trim leading and trailing spaces from account text fields.
Loading a Hive by Referral ID now shows only that account and its downline tree, without exposing uplines.
Hive sync now allows external Invited By IDs that are not yet stored in the Hive table, while still validating known parent account types.
Hive sync errors now explain the exact cloud sync issue, including missing or incompatible Invited By ID parents.
The app update notification now stays above Hive and other active tool screens.
Loading a Hive by Referral ID now shows a visible success or not-found toast.
Successful Hive sync now shows a clear green confirmation toast.
[NEW] Hive structures can now be exported to JSON and imported again for offline updates before cloud syncing.
Sync now can publish a local Hive to the cloud database when no cloud copy exists yet, making it loadable by Referral ID.
Adding Hive accounts now starts with an empty Referral ID so users can enter the exact account ID.
Adding Hive accounts now starts with a blank name and reuses the last selected account country by default.
The first loaded Hive account can now enter an Invited By ID so a new personal structure can link into an existing Hive.
[NEW] Hive now has a Clear sample option plus manual and automatic cloud sync for structure changes added elsewhere.
Hive account editing now labels Amount as Personal investment and adds a Total turnover field backed by the database schema.
[NEW] My income now includes its own start-date calendar and can automatically set up compounding, switch to simple mode on the target date, and run the main projection.
Breakdown percentage labels now match the selected interval, with weekly, monthly, quarterly, and yearly percentages calculated for that full interval.
Hive opening is more reliable with a direct module fallback, plus improved tree dragging in focused and isolated views.
Planning tools received darker card styling so the tools stand out more clearly.
Version checking now shows a polished update popup with release notes while the browser remains open.
